mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in real personal consumption expenditures (RSAFS) signal robust underlying demand growth, which tends to drive corporate earnings and forward equity returns higher over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month changes show a negative correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) within a quarter often precedes or coincides with economic slowdowns, which erodes corporate profitability and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period
evidence_refs: fact:historical recessions preceded by unemployment rises
failure_condition: In more than half of triggered events, the 60-day forward return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When both consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) is elevated and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is rising, the combination tightens financial conditions and depresses valuation multiples, leading to negative short-term forward returns over a 5-day window.
regime_hint: monetary tightening or high inflation environment
failure_condition: The joint signal's rank IC over rolling 1-year windows is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: In early economic recovery phases, robust payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) reinforces income expectations and consumer confidence, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In NBER-dated early recovery periods, positive payroll surprises lead to negative 20-day returns more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent expansion in industrial production (INDPRO) signals strong manufacturing activity and broader economic growth, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO 6-month growth rate has a rank IC <= 0 with 60-day forward returns over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts (HOUST) below the 1400K level indicates a housing market trough, which historically precedes a rebound in residential investment and economic activity, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST drops below 1400 thousand units (monthly, seasonally adjusted annual rate)
failure_condition: After HOUST crosses below 1400, the average 20-day forward return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating GDP growth combined with rising unemployment is a classic recession indicator, historically leading to negative equity returns over a quarter. This interaction of demand and labor data captures business cycle turning points.
failure_condition: When both GDP growth is negative quarter-over-quarter and unemployment is rising month-over-month, 60-day forward returns are positive more than 30% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: During a sustained monetary tightening cycle, elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) compress valuation multiples and increase discount rates, resulting in negative short-term (5-trading-day) equity returns.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS rising over past 6 months)
failure_condition: In periods identified as tightening cycles, the 5-day forward return is positive in more than 60% of instances.
